nodes.context: Major Mexican drug cartel, subject of internal conflict.
Faction of the Sinaloa Cartel led by sons of Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán.
Faction of the Sinaloa Cartel led by Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada and his son Mayito Flaco.
Armed group aligned with La Mayiza, operating in Durango and Zacatecas.
Chapitos-affiliated elements involved in clashes with Grupo Flechas.
Armed group aligned with MZ/MF faction, from Baja California.
Allies of La Mayiza, based in Durango.
Group allied with Los Cazadores, operating in Sonora.
Group allied with Los Salazar, conducting armed patrols in Sonora.
Mexican drug cartel that formed alliance with Chapitos in May 2025.
Group aligned with Los Chapitos.
Independent Sonora Cartel faction.
Chapitos-affiliated group.
Chapitos-affiliated group.
Group aligned with MZ/MF faction.
Criminal figure who formed alliance with La Mayiza in early May 2025.
Major Mexican drug cartel, currently in internal conflict.
Faction of the Sinaloa Cartel led by sons of Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán.
Faction of the Sinaloa Cartel aligned with Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada and/or Mayito Flaco.
MF-aligned group within the Sinaloa Cartel conflict.
Armed group aligned with the Mayitos (MZ/MF faction).
Mexican drug cartel, allied with Los Chapitos.
Another name for Los Chapitos faction.
Another name for MZ/MF faction.
Armed faction aligned with the MZ faction.
Faction of the Gulf Cartel, reportedly in support of CJNG.
Mexican drug cartel; its Los Metros faction supported CJNG.
Mexican drug cartel, clashed with Los Cabrera.
Faction of the Beltrán Leyva Organization, aligned with MZ/MF.
Mexican drug cartel; the Guasave Cartel is a faction of it.
Chapitos faction group.
Armed group aligned with MZ/MF, led by Aureliano Guzmán Loera.
Mexican criminal group, clashed with Los Cabrera.
Armed group aligned with MZ/MF.
Special forces of Chapo Isidro (Guasave Cartel/BLO).
Chapitos faction group.
Group whose members were executed by Chapiza-CJNG alliance.
MF/MZ-affiliated sicario group.
Group allied with CJNG and Los Chapitos.
Sicario group aligned with MZ/MF.
Group affiliated with Chapo Isidro (Guasave Cartel/BLO).
Major Mexican drug cartel involved in internal conflict.
Faction of the Sinaloa Cartel led by sons of Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán.
Faction of the Sinaloa Cartel loyal to Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada and his allies.

edges.evidence_quote: The event triggered a formal schism within the cartel and escalated into a multi-front war between the MZ/MF faction and Los Chapitos
edges.context: The two factions are in open armed conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: armed confrontations between Flechas aligned groups and Chapitos affiliated elements, often referred to as 'Los Cascos' — continued intermittently throughout the Sinaloa highlands
edges.context: Grupo Flechas and Los Cascos engaged in intermittent armed clashes.

edges.evidence_quote: Mayito Flaco reportedly authorized Flechas-aligned forces to expand into southern Sinaloa, directly challenging Chapitos control
edges.context: Grupo Flechas is aligned with La Mayiza and acts on its behalf.

edges.evidence_quote: armed units aligned with the MZ/MF faction redeployed to Sinaloa to engage Los Chapitos. These included 'Los Rusos' from Baja California
edges.context: Los Rusos are aligned with La Mayiza and fight alongside them.

edges.evidence_quote: a small plane, reportedly linked to the Los Chapitos faction, dropped four bombs on Vascogil, Durango, a stronghold of the Cabrera Sarabia Organization, allies of La Mayiza
edges.context: Los Cabrera are allies of La Mayiza.

edges.evidence_quote: Video footage showed 'Los Cazadores' allied with Los Salazar, conducting armed patrols in El Claro, Sonora
edges.context: Los Cazadores and Los Salazar are allied and conduct joint patrols.

edges.evidence_quote: Chapitos form alliance with CJNG in May 2025
edges.context: Los Chapitos and CJNG formed an alliance in May 2025.

edges.evidence_quote: 'El Chapo Isidro' forms alliance with La Mayiza in Early May 2025
edges.context: El Chapo Isidro formed an alliance with La Mayiza in early May 2025.

edges.evidence_quote: two members of 'Los Monguis', a group aligned with Los Chapitos, were killed
edges.context: Los Monguis are aligned with Los Chapitos.

edges.evidence_quote: a Chapitos-affiliated individual—possibly linked to Los Deltas or Los Pelones
edges.context: Los Deltas are affiliated with Los Chapitos.

edges.evidence_quote: a Chapitos-affiliated individual—possibly linked to Los Deltas or Los Pelones
edges.context: Los Pelones are affiliated with Los Chapitos.

edges.evidence_quote: sicarios affiliated with 'Gente de Lupe Tapia', aligned with MZ/MF
edges.context: Gente de Lupe Tapia is aligned with La Mayiza.

edges.evidence_quote: a Jeep Gladiator displaying extensive gunfire damage was found in Caborca, Sonora, reportedly targeted by Los Costeños, an Independent Sonora Cartel faction
edges.context: Los Costeños targeted a vehicle, but no direct relationship with Los Chapitos is stated; the quote only mentions Los Costeños as an independent faction. No edge.

edges.evidence_quote: The Sinaloa War is an ongoing internal armed conflict within the Sinaloa Cartel, centered in northwestern Mexico. The conflict began on 9 September 2024, following the transfer of Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada to United States authorities by members of the Chapitos faction.
edges.context: Los Chapitos and MZ/MF faction are in armed conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: On 6 January 2025, two bodies reportedly belonging to the Rugrats, an MF-aligned group, were left with narcomessages beside the letras monument in Costa Rica, Sinaloa, with Los Chapitos believed to be responsible.
edges.context: Los Chapitos are believed responsible for killing Rugrats members.

edges.evidence_quote: On 23 January 2025, Grupo Flechas, aligned with the Mayitos, were reported to have captured CJNG members in Villanueva, Zacatecas.
edges.context: Grupo Flechas captured CJNG members; CJNG is allied with Chapitos, so Grupo Flechas is in conflict with Chapitos.

edges.evidence_quote: On 23 January 2025, Grupo Flechas, aligned with the Mayitos, were reported to have captured CJNG members in Villanueva, Zacatecas.
edges.context: Grupo Flechas captured CJNG members.

edges.evidence_quote: On 9 June 2025, CJNG and Chapitos members were photographed posing together in front of the letras monument in Cacalótan, Sinaloa, further suggesting an alliance.
edges.context: CJNG and Los Chapitos are allied.

edges.evidence_quote: On 31 January 2025, video footage showed clashes between La Chapiza and La Mayiza in El Palmito, Sinaloa.
edges.context: La Chapiza is another name for Los Chapitos, so this is the same entity.

edges.evidence_quote: On 31 January 2025, video footage showed clashes between La Chapiza and La Mayiza in El Palmito, Sinaloa.
edges.context: La Mayiza is another name for MZ/MF faction.

edges.evidence_quote: On 31 January 2025, video footage showed clashes between La Chapiza and La Mayiza in El Palmito, Sinaloa.
edges.context: La Chapiza (Chapitos) and La Mayiza (MZ/MF) are in conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: On 18 February 2025, vehicles belonging to Los Cabreras, aligned with the MZ faction, were recorded traveling on the federal free highway in Pueblo Nuevo, Durango.
edges.context: Los Cabreras is aligned with MZ/MF faction.

edges.evidence_quote: On 26 February 2025, clashes were reported between MZ/MF forces and CJNG in Zonteco, Nayarit.
edges.context: MZ/MF forces and CJNG are in conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: On 14 March 2025, gunmen from the Los Metros faction of the Gulf Cartel were observed operating armored vehicles in Zacatecas, reportedly in support of CJNG.
edges.context: Los Metros faction supports CJNG.

edges.evidence_quote: On 14 March 2025, gunmen from the Los Metros faction of the Gulf Cartel were observed operating armored vehicles in Zacatecas, reportedly in support of CJNG.
edges.context: Los Metros is a faction of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: On 9–10 July, clashes between the New Juárez Cartel and Los Cabrera, an armed faction aligned with La Mayiza, resulted in at least 23 fatalities.
edges.context: New Juárez Cartel and Los Cabrera are in conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Early May 2025, the Guasave Cartel, a faction of the Beltrán Leyva Organization led by Fausto Isidro Meza Flores (“Chapo Isidro”), abandoned its previous neutral stance. Armed clashes were reported in Mocorito and Guamúchil, and the BLO formally aligned with the MZ/MF faction.
edges.context: Guasave Cartel (BLO) formally aligned with MZ/MF faction.

edges.evidence_quote: Early May 2025, the Guasave Cartel, a faction of the Beltrán Leyva Organization led by Fausto Isidro Meza Flores (“Chapo Isidro”), abandoned its previous neutral stance.
edges.context: Guasave Cartel is a faction of the Beltrán Leyva Organization.

edges.evidence_quote: On 5 May 2025, fighting occurred in Mocorito, Sinaloa between Numerada (Chapitos) and forces aligned with Chapo Isidro, although reports of the participants remained unconfirmed.
edges.context: Numerada is a Chapitos group.

edges.evidence_quote: On 5 May 2025, fighting occurred in Mocorito, Sinaloa between Numerada (Chapitos) and forces aligned with Chapo Isidro, although reports of the participants remained unconfirmed.
edges.context: Forces aligned with Chapo Isidro (Guasave Cartel) fought Numerada (Chapitos).

edges.evidence_quote: On 6 May 2025, armed men belonging to "Gente Del Guano" seen patrolling in Badiraguato, Sinaloa.
edges.context: Gente Del Guano is aligned with MZ/MF.

edges.evidence_quote: On 27 May 2025, clashes occurred in Aldama – Ojinaga, Chihuahua, between "Los Cabrera" of MZ/MF and La Línea, as MZ/MF attempted to advance in the area.
edges.context: La Linea clashed with Los Cabrera (MZ/MF), so La Linea is likely allied with Chapitos or independent; but no direct edge between Chapitos and La Linea is stated.

edges.evidence_quote: On 27 May 2025, clashes occurred in Aldama – Ojinaga, Chihuahua, between "Los Cabrera" of MZ/MF and La Línea, as MZ/MF attempted to advance in the area.
edges.context: Los Cabrera (MZ/MF) clashed with La Linea.

edges.evidence_quote: On 26 May 2025, members of "Los Rusos", aligned with the MZ/MF, entered the town of El Pozo, Sinaloa, previously controlled by Los Chapitos.
edges.context: Los Rusos are aligned with MZ/MF.

edges.evidence_quote: On 26 May 2025, members of "Los Rusos", aligned with the MZ/MF, entered the town of El Pozo, Sinaloa, previously controlled by Los Chapitos.
edges.context: Los Rusos (MZ/MF) entered Chapitos-controlled area, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: On 15 June 2025, video footage captured "Vitache Special Forces" of Chapo Isidro engaged in combat against "Los Ranas" of Chapitos in the Tierra Blanca neighborhood of Culiacán, Sinaloa.
edges.context: Los Vitaches are special forces of Chapo Isidro (Guasave Cartel).

edges.evidence_quote: On 15 June 2025, video footage captured "Vitache Special Forces" of Chapo Isidro engaged in combat against "Los Ranas" of Chapitos in the Tierra Blanca neighborhood of Culiacán, Sinaloa.
edges.context: Los Ranas are a Chapitos group.

edges.evidence_quote: On 15 June 2025, video footage captured "Vitache Special Forces" of Chapo Isidro engaged in combat against "Los Ranas" of Chapitos in the Tierra Blanca neighborhood of Culiacán, Sinaloa.
edges.context: Vitache Special Forces (Guasave Cartel) fought Los Ranas (Chapitos).

edges.evidence_quote: On 16 June 2025, Los Vitaches and Los Rusos were observed arriving at El Tamarindo, north of Culiacán, Sinaloa, to consolidate control.
edges.context: Los Vitaches and Los Rusos (both MZ/MF-aligned) operate together.

edges.evidence_quote: On 24 June 2025, the Chapiza–CJNG alliance reportedly executed two suspected turncoats from "Los Avendaño".
edges.context: Chapiza-CJNG alliance executed members of Los Avendaño.

edges.evidence_quote: On 24 June 2025, the Chapiza–CJNG alliance reportedly executed two suspected turncoats from "Los Avendaño".
edges.context: Chapiza-CJNG alliance executed members of Los Avendaño.

edges.evidence_quote: On 31 July 2025, MF/MZ-affiliated "La Gente del Tigre" sicarios patrolled San Juan, Sinaloa, near the letras monument.
edges.context: La Gente del Tigre is affiliated with MZ/MF.

edges.evidence_quote: On 15 August 2025, a convoy of MZ/MF-affiliated sicarios was observed on the road near El Huérfano, Chihuahua, reportedly searching for NCDJ members.
edges.context: MZ/MF sicarios searching for NCDJ (New Juárez Cartel) members indicates conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: On 15 October 2025, GDG–Los Calabazas sicarios were seen in Arroyo Seco, Sinaloa, repainting graffiti over previous Chapitos markings.
edges.context: GDG–Los Calabazas are aligned with MZ/MF.

edges.evidence_quote: On 26 October 2025, a dronero affiliated with Los Músicos / Chapo Isidro reportedly dropped a munition on a Gente del Guano-affiliated vehicle southwest of La Tuna, Sinaloa.
edges.context: Los Músicos are affiliated with Chapo Isidro (Guasave Cartel).

edges.evidence_quote: On 26 October 2025, a dronero affiliated with Los Músicos / Chapo Isidro reportedly dropped a munition on a Gente del Guano-affiliated vehicle southwest of La Tuna, Sinaloa.
edges.context: Los Músicos (Guasave Cartel) attacked Gente del Guano (MZ/MF-aligned), indicating conflict between Guasave Cartel and Gente del Guano.

edges.evidence_quote: On 9 September 2025, MF/MF-affiliated Grupo Flechas sicarios were documented on the streets of Pánuco, Sinaloa.
edges.context: Grupo Flechas is affiliated with MZ/MF.

edges.evidence_quote: On 17 December 2025, intense clashes were reported in Escuinapa, Sinaloa, between rival armed groups, likely involving Fuerzas Especiales Unión (FEU)— CJNG and Los Chapitos—against forces of "Grupo Flechas" of Mayito Flaco (MZ/MF).
edges.context: FEU is allied with CJNG and Los Chapitos.

edges.evidence_quote: On 17 December 2025, intense clashes were reported in Escuinapa, Sinaloa, between rival armed groups, likely involving Fuerzas Especiales Unión (FEU)— CJNG and Los Chapitos—against forces of "Grupo Flechas" of Mayito Flaco (MZ/MF).
edges.context: FEU is allied with CJNG.

edges.evidence_quote: On 17 December 2025, intense clashes were reported in Escuinapa, Sinaloa, between rival armed groups, likely involving Fuerzas Especiales Unión (FEU)— CJNG and Los Chapitos—against forces of "Grupo Flechas" of Mayito Flaco (MZ/MF).
edges.context: FEU and Grupo Flechas are on opposing sides in clashes.

edges.evidence_quote: The conflict began on 9 September 2024, following the transfer of Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada to United States authorities by members of the Chapitos faction. The event triggered a formal schism within the cartel and escalated into a multi-front war between the MZ/MF faction and Los Chapitos
edges.context: The two factions are engaged in an internal armed conflict within the Sinaloa Cartel.
